channel.title: Ah Beng Podcast
channel.itunes:author: Beng
channel.description: AI Daily Brief (ad-free), ChinaTalk deep-dives, and Viewpoints (觀點) Chinese-language content — all in one feed.

item.title: AI Daily Brief — Fable is Back: Here's What You Should Try First (2026-07-01)
item.description: Fable 5 returns after 19 days — Anthropic cleared by Commerce, export controls lifted. Plus: OpenAI's secret 50% inference cost cut, Base44 builds its own model (Base1), AWS drops $1B on forward-deployed engineers, Claude Tag coming to Microsoft Teams, SpaceX offers half-price Starlink to Memphis neighbors, Claude Sonnet 5 ships (expensive but agentic), and a practical playbook for using Fable 5 during its free week — strategy and rubric-driven writing, not just coding. Ad-free clean read.

item.title: AI Daily Brief — How Big Is the AI Economy? (2026-06-30)
item.description: The AI economy is real, big, and fast — $175B annualized run rate growing 3x faster than any IT wave before it. Exponential View audited 1,000+ AI companies: $110B banked over 12 months, CapEx of $848B this year, and quarterly revenue now exceeds CapEx depreciation. Plus headlines: Fable KYC identity verification rumors, Senator Warner's agent neutrality bill, California's half-price Claude deal, Amazon loses its Anthropic sweetheart deal, Meta bans coding agents to dodge distillation, Google caps Meta's Gemini usage, AWS hikes GPU prices 20%, Ramageddon memory crisis, and more. Ad-free clean read.

item.title: AI Daily Brief — Mythos Comes Back But Not for Everyone (2026-06-29)
item.description: Mythos returns for ~100 vetted partners and GPT-5.6 ships as Sol, Terra, and Luna — but both are locked behind a government access list. Frontier AI is now a de facto licensing regime run on Howard Lutnick's discretion. The vibe shift is real, sympathy for the government's predicament is growing, and the world on the other side of the Fable/Mythos bans won't be the one we had before. Plus: Sol benchmarks, Meter's cheating-rate evaluation, China's GLM 5.2 cybersecurity claims, Coinbase halving its AI bill with Chinese open models, and open-weight models holding a steady 3-6 month gap. Ad-free clean read.

item.title: Software Is Becoming Marketing (2026-06-30)
item.description: As AI makes software trivial to build, software itself is losing value and becoming like marketing — a commodity rather than a moat. We explore four key implications: (1) Lower barrier to entry makes software a lower-respect field, leading to pay compression — only the proven best command a premium. (2) Optionality kills commitment — like dating apps, too many choices lead to less satisfaction. (3) The middle class of software products disappears — good enough vendors die, hyper-niche disposable apps explode, and aggregators win through trust and distribution. (4) Sell services, not products — Sequoia's next $1T company will be "a software company masquerading as a services firm." When AI handles the mundane work, what's left is judgment, brand, and proven track record. Based on Tereza Tizkova's article "Software Abundance."

item.title: AI Daily Brief — The Capability Overhang Playbook (2026-06-28)
item.description: We're in a forced, involuntary AI pause — GPT-5.6, Sonnet 5, and Gemini 3.5 Pro all slipped, and the Fable lockout has the industry frozen. NLW reframes the lull as opportunity: today's models already hold far more capability than most people extract. The playbook covers mapping your personal capability gap, building eval portfolios, portable context assets, experimenting with harnesses, exploring model independence, and at the org level — better training, incentives, and measurement. Plus advanced patterns: agent loops, MCP servers, and reusable skills. Ad-free clean read.

item.title: 觀點│正經龍鳳配 — 高市快被中國逼瘋了！川普快被伊朗捏死了！ (2026-06-29)
item.description: 2026.06.29【觀點│正經龍鳳配】高市快被中國逼瘋了！川普快被伊朗捏死了！ — Chinese-language geopolitics commentary from the Viewpoints (觀點) channel. Discussion covers China-Taiwan tensions (Kaohsiung city pressure), Trump-Iran ceasefire brinkmanship, and latest international affairs analysis.

item.title: AI Daily Brief — Botsitting: The Work Draining AI Gains (2026-06-26)
item.description: A new Glean / Work AI Institute report names the hidden labor eating the AI productivity dividend — "bot sitting" — and its uglier cousin, "bot shitting." NLW digs in because, unlike most 2026 studies, he thinks the agentic shift would amplify these findings, not erase them. Workers save 11 hours a week with AI but burn 6.4 of them bot sitting. Only 13% of organizations say AI made them significantly better. The differentiator isn't the AI you buy; it's the human infrastructure you build. Ad-free clean read.

item.title: How AI Is Reshaping Work: Inside Anthropic's Economic Index (June 2026)
item.description: A deep dive into Anthropic's June 2026 Economic Index report — the most comprehensive look at how AI is actually being used in the real world. Three key areas: (1) The rhythms of AI usage — how Claude queries mirror daily life, from morning news checks at 7 AM to recipe requests at 6 PM to sleep advice at 5 AM. Weekend usage shifts personal, with startup business planning peaking on Saturdays and Sundays. (2) What people produce — explanations lead at 17%, followed by documents and reports at 15%. Compute cost tracks economic value: higher-wage tasks consume more tokens, but humans remain in the loop. Claude Code shows dramatically higher AI autonomy than chat, even on the same model. (3) The survey of 9,700 users reveals over a third expect AI to handle most of their job within 12 months. Counterintuitively, the heaviest AI delegators are the most optimistic about their career prospects. Early-career workers express the most concern. Original report: anthropic.com/research/economic-index-june-2026-report

item.title: AI Daily Brief — CEO-Led AI Gets 3X the ROI (2026-06-25)
item.description: OpenAI unveils its first in-house chip codenamed Jalapeño, built with Broadcom in just 9 months. Anthropic accuses Alibaba of the largest distillation attack ever — 29 million accesses via 25,000 fraudulent accounts. Prediction markets swing wildly on Fable 5 return signals. Claude Tag draws vendor lock-in fears. DeepMind keeps bleeding senior researchers. Micron's blowout earnings flip bubble jitters with 445% YoY growth. And a KPMG survey shows CEOs who own AI strategy see 3X the ROI. Ad-free clean read.

item.title: AI Daily Brief — 5 Ways Claude Tag Could Change How You Use AI (2026-06-24)
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Tag puts a full Claude Code teammate inside Slack — a third major redesign of how we interact with LLMs. Plus a customer sues the US over the Fable ban, Washington pressures Meta on voluntary model review, Commerce turns from models to robots, xAI adds the goal primitive to Grok Build, ByteDance's Seedance 2.5 doubles clip length to 30s with 4K, and KPMG research on high-impact AI users. Ad-free clean read.

item.title: AI Daily Brief — The Right Way to Deal With AI Data Centers (2026-06-23)
item.description: The data center fight needs a middle path between rollover and pitchforks. Plus: Mythos/NSA correction, OpenAI's GPT 5.5 Cyber and Daybreak initiative, Patch the Planet, Five Eyes AI cyber-risk alert, Trump quantum computing executive orders, SpaceX $6.3B Reflection AI deal, $200B Google market cap drop from researcher exits, and a deep dive into data center water and electricity numbers. Ad-free clean read.

item.title: 觀點│正經龍鳳配 — 盟友内訌！惡言相向！美國體系，加速瓦解！
item.description: 正經龍鳳配 (2026-06-22): 盟友内訌！惡言相向！美國體系，加速瓦解！ — Audio extracted directly from YouTube. Topics include US alliance infighting and the accelerating disintegration of the American system.

item.title: 觀點│唐湘龍時間 — 為什麼只有新加坡怕那部電影？
item.description: 唐湘龍時間 (2026-06-22): 為什麼只有新加坡怕那部電影？ — Audio extracted directly from YouTube. Topics include Singapore's unique concerns about a controversial film.

item.title: Transmission Dominance with Chinese Characteristics
item.description: A summary of Dana Golden's ChinaTalk deep-dive comparing the US and China transmission buildout — and what it means for AI, energy security, and the future of grid infrastructure. China has built more HVDC in 15 years than the US has in its entire history. Covers the scale of the gap, why "China builds fast" is a shallow explanation, the fragmented US regulatory patchwork, two fundamentally different grid design philosophies, the Grain Belt Express saga, market mechanism irony (US has software, China has hardware), the broken timescale mismatch, batteries vs transmission as complements not substitutes, supply chain concentration in HVDC converters, advantages of the American decentralized approach, direct implications for AI data center buildout, and whether the gap is insurmountable. Original article: 2026-06-21.

item.title: Yes, Transformers Are a Problem (Full Reading)
item.description: The full article by Dana Golden, economist at Argonne National Labs, read in its entirety. A comprehensive deep-dive into the power electronics supply chain crisis — gallium, wide-bandgap semiconductors, HVDC, solid-state transformers, Wolfspeed's collapse, and policy recommendations. Original article: 2026-06-20.

item.title: Yes, Transformers Are a Problem (Summary)
item.description: A condensed ~25-minute summary of Dana Golden's ChinaTalk deep-dive on the power electronics supply chain crisis. Covers the key arguments: why fixing the transformer shortage won't fix the grid, how gallium is the real chokepoint (99% China-controlled), why most "critical minerals" anxiety is misplaced, the Wolfspeed cautionary tale, NVIDIA's 800V data center revolution, the four-company HVDC oligopoly, and ten concrete policy recommendations. Original article: 2026-06-20.

item.title: Why AI Users Are Raving About GLM 5.2
item.description: GLM 5.2 just did what no Chinese open-weight model has done before — it survived first contact with real usage. As power users rave and a Nobel laureate flees DeepMind, NLW separates fact from innuendo on the Fable saga and asks whether the two-horse race is truly over. Covers the Mythos/NSA red-team exercise context, Trump's conciliatory tone on Anthropic, John Jumper leaving DeepMind for Anthropic, plummeting DeepMind morale, GLM 5.2's DeepSeek R1 moment, why Chinese open models usually fade but GLM 5.2 is different, Design Arena benchmarks vs Fable 5, the real cost of running GLM 5.2, and why the two-horse race narrative is broken. Original episode: 2026-06-22.

item.title: Your Company Doesn't Need an AI Strategy
item.description: White House–Anthropic talks may finally be thawing, Bernie Sanders unveils a $7T AI sovereign wealth fund, Accenture gets hammered as the market prices in AI disruption, and Satya Nadella's viral "token capital" essay redefines the enterprise AI conversation: you don't need an AI strategy, you need a learning loop you actually own. Covers the Fable outage fallout, Nadella's full essay, Microsoft Frontier Tuning, the applied AI layer, and Harvey's vision for law firms. Original episode: 2026-06-19.

item.title: How Chinese Make Sense of the AI Future
item.description: A ChinaTalk deep-dive into how Chinese society is grappling with AI's impact on workers, labor politics, and the national psyche. Covers the physical-vs-digital workforce divide, young people's anxiety about white-collar automation, why state-sector workers are insulated, how courts and state media mediate tensions, why data center protests don't exist in China, robot tax debates, and the deeper crisis of meaning beneath AI anxiety. Based on original reporting by Irene Zhang and Nick Corvino for ChinaTalk. Original article: 2026-06-19.

item.title: The Models Trying to Fill the Fable Gap
item.description: With Fable still dark, the G7 turns into a global plea for access to US frontier models — and enterprises stop waiting, MacGyvering Chinese open weights, compound APIs, and smart routing into something that gets close to Fable-level performance. Covers the G7 AI summit, Noam Shazeer leaving Google for OpenAI, OpenAI sunsetting Pulse, GLM 5.2 beating Fable on benchmarks, Microsoft eyeing DeepSeek for Copilot, Cursor Composer 2.5, OpenRouter Fusion, and Harvey's worker-advisor architecture. Original episode: 2026-06-18.

item.title: 觀點│正經龍鳳配 — 賠了夫人又折兵！灰頭土臉 80 歲！
item.description: 正經龍鳳配 (2026-06-15): 賠了夫人又折兵！灰頭土臉 80 歲！— Audio extracted directly from YouTube.

item.title: 觀點│畫龍點睛 EP117 — 中國潛艦技術大爆發
item.description: EP117 (2026-06-16): 中國潛艦技術大爆發 — in-depth discussion on China's submarine technology breakthroughs. Audio extracted directly from YouTube.

item.title: Why Only AI Training Can Save the Economy
item.description: A Big Think episode on why the relationship between AI lab revenue growth and infrastructure build-out is the defining relationship of the American economy — and why mass-scale AI training is the only thing that can satisfy both labs' need for token growth and enterprises' need for ROI. Original episode: 2026-06-16.

item.title: A Big Shift in the AI Race
item.description: Anthropic's Mythos/Fable shutdown drags into its fifth day with no resolution — while Elon Musk uses SpaceX's blockbuster IPO to swallow Cursor for $60B and reshape the model race. AI is being pulled ever deeper under national security control, and the race just got more dynamic than anyone expected. Original episode: 2026-06-17.

item.title: The Fable 5 Crisis Continues
item.description: The weekend's Anthropic-vs-White House standoff over Fable 5 grinds into Monday with no resolution — dueling press releases, the Amazon jailbreak report, the wellness-retreat meme, and the dawning realization that this fight was never going to be settled by engineers. Original episode: 2026-06-15.

item.title: Fable 5 Shut Down by US Government
item.description: A national-security directive forced Anthropic to abruptly cut off Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all foreign nationals worldwide. Original episode: 2026-06-13.
